description:
# Interpreter tuple construction + projection

`ir_interp.rs::eval_prim` handles `Const`, `BinOp`, `Extern`,
`MakeBitstring`, `ConstBitstring`, `MakeClosure`, `TypeTest`,
and falls through to a "prim {:?} not yet supported (lands in
fz-ul4.23.5.3+)" error for everything else.

## Symptom

```fz
fn main() do
  e = {:num, 7}
  print(e)
end
```

On `fz interp` errors with exit 75 and:
`prim Discriminant(9) not yet supported (lands in fz-ul4.23.5.3+)`

(`Discriminant(9)` = `Prim::MakeTuple` per the variant order
in `src/fz_ir.rs:153`.)

## Fix

Add arms to `ir_interp.rs::eval_prim`:

- `Prim::MakeTuple(args)` — alloc a Struct heap value with N fields,
  store each captured FzValue at offset HEADER + i * SLOT_BYTES.
  Mirror the JIT codegen (`ir_codegen.rs` MakeTuple arm) for layout
  agreement; both paths share `fz_runtime::heap`.
- `Prim::TupleField(v, i)` — load FzValue at HEADER + i * SLOT_BYTES
  from `v`'s unboxed pointer.

## Acceptance

- `fz interp` runs:
  ```fz
  fn main() do
    e = {:num, 7}
    print(e)
    {a, b} = e
    print(a)
    print(b)
  end
  ```
  with output `{:num, 7}\n:num\n7\n`.
- All existing fixtures still pass on the `interp` leg.
- Standalone — does NOT yet enable multi-clause tuple dispatch; that
  needs descr.tuples in TypeTest (separate ticket).
title: ir_interp: implement Prim::MakeTuple + Prim::TupleField
